mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors seek higher-risk assets.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative in 60%+ of periods when FEDFUNDS declines for 3+ consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 5% indicates tightening labor markets that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through earnings momentum.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 50%+ of instances when UNRATE declines below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal weakening demand that pressures corporate margins, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in 55%+ of periods when both RSAFS and HOUST decline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 3-month moving average indicates expanding capacity utilization that raises inflationary pressures, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through Fed rate hike expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 50%+ of instances when INDPRO exceeds its 3-month MA.

mechanism_narrative: A 20bp+ increase in the federal funds rate combined with stable CPI (CPIAUCSL < 0.2% MoM) signals effective monetary policy that stabilizes inflation expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through risk-on sentiment.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in 55%+ of periods meeting the FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly gain in payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding 200k combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE > 0.3% MoM) indicates labor market overheating that prompts Fed intervention, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through volatility spikes.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in 50%+ of instances meeting the PAYEMS/UNRATE trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) decline below 2% signals economic slowdown that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through earnings downgrade risks.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 55%+ of periods when GDPC1 declines below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100 combined with rising oil prices (not in catalog) would signal supply shocks, but since oil isn't available, this hypothesis uses INDPRO alone to predict negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through inflationary pressure.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 50%+ of instances when INDPRO declines below 100.
